title: Deva Raja
wikitext:
{{Infobox
| title      = Deva Raja
| header1    = Identity
| label2     = Full Name
| data2      = Deva Raja
| label3     = Era
| data3      = [[Terra Fathos]] ([[Solar Cycle 25]])
| label4     = Role
| data4      = '''Main Character''' of [[Terra Fathos]]
| label5     = Partner MC
| data5      = [[Tara Van Dekar]]
| label6     = Affiliation
| data6      = —
}}

'''Deva Raja''' is one of the two '''Main Characters''' of the '''[[Terra Fathos]]''' timespan in the '''[[Natura]]''' franchise, alongside '''[[Tara Van Dekar]]'''.

== Overview ==
While [[Tara Van Dekar]] carries the spiritual legacy of the [[Rosicrucian]] tradition and the [[Cosmic Cypher Sacred Scroll]], Deva Raja represents a complementary force — an Indian man whose destiny intertwines with Tara's across [[Solar Cycle 25]].

Together, Tara and Deva Raja anchor the [[Terra Fathos]] era as its dual Main Characters. Their intertwined narratives persist across later timespans: both '''[[Tara Van Dekar]]''' and '''Deva Raja''' carry forward through the [[Terra Vetus]] and [[Terra Novus]] timelines, their influence echoing across generations.

== In the Franchise ==
In the '''[[FusionGirl (franchise)|Natura franchise]]''' architecture:
* Deva Raja is one of 2 Main Characters of [[Terra Fathos]] (alongside [[Tara Van Dekar]])
* The 6 secondary characters of Fathos (including [[Jono Tho'ra]], [[Aurora Tho'ra]], [[Hiro Toshino]], [[Samurider]], [[Gabriel Valenti]], and [[Stephen]]) orbit around Tara and Deva Raja's central narrative
* Deva Raja persists through later timelines — his role and influence extend into [[Terra Vetus]] and [[Terra Novus]]

== Player Consciousness ==
In [[Terra Fathos]], the guiding super-consciousness that the player embodies takes the form of an '''[[Angel|Angelic]] presence''' — divine guidance expressed through visions, sacred signs, and environmental synchronicities. The angels watch over and guide Tara and Deva Raja through the mythic and spiritual conflicts of the origin era.

== Name Significance ==
The name ''Deva Raja'' carries significance: ''Deva'' (divine being) and ''Raja'' (king/ruler) in Sanskrit — "Divine King" — suggesting a figure of profound spiritual authority.

== See Also ==
* [[Tara Van Dekar]] — partner Main Character of Terra Fathos
* [[Terra Fathos]] — the first timespan
* [[The Terra Fathos Chronicles]]
* [[Natura]] — the overarching franchise
* [[Characters]]

title: Device
wikitext:
A [[Device]] refers to a distinct, individual unit or piece of equipment designed to perform a particular task. 

Devices can vary widely in complexity and application, ranging from simple everyday gadgets to sophisticated [[Electronic Instruments]]. 

Each device serves a specific purpose, contributing to the overall functionality of a [[System]] or fulfilling a standalone function. 

Exploring the features and applications of different devices is key to understanding their role within various systems. 

Examples of devices include common ones like [[Smartphones]] and specialized ones like [[Medical Devices]].

title: Dimension
wikitext:
Dimensions are ways that elements of physics can be measured within a [[Universe]]

All versions of [[Human]] [[Earth|Earths]] live within a 12 Dimensional Holographic Universal [[Cosmos]]

* [[1st Dimension]]
* [[2nd Dimension]]
* [[3rd Dimension]]
* [[4th Dimension]]
* [[5th Dimension]]
* [[6th Dimension]]
* [[7th Dimension]]
* [[8th Dimension]]
* [[9th Dimension]]
* [[10th Dimension]]
* [[11th Dimension]]
* [[12th Dimension]]


Each Dimension of a [[Universe]] has a corresponding [[Reality]] called a [[Realm]] as well as a [[Plane]] of [[Existence]]

title: Dimensional Rifts
wikitext:
'''Dimensional Rifts''' are interdimensional tears in the fabric of reality that represent one of the '''central threats''' in the [[FusionGirl (franchise)|FusionGirl]] universe. They are exploited by [[The Nefarium]], caused by [[Draken Seforthi]] activity, and serve as both '''obstacles''' and '''opportunities''' throughout gameplay.

== Nature ==
Dimensional Rifts are unstable breaches between realities — points where the barriers between dimensions have weakened or collapsed. They manifest as visible distortions in space, often accompanied by:
* '''Psionic interference''' disruptive to [[PsiNet]] operations
* '''Environmental hazards''' including energy surges and terrain distortion
* '''Enemy incursions''' — interdimensional entities pouring through from other realities
* '''Temporal anomalies''' affecting local time flow

== Causes ==
Rifts can form through several mechanisms:
* '''[[Draken Seforthi]] activity''' — their consumption of planetary energy weakens dimensional barriers
* '''[[The Nefarium|Nefarium]] exploitation''' — agents deliberately destabilize rifts to create [[Interdimensional Portals]]
* '''[[Time Wars]] residue''' — the chaotic temporal manipulation of [[Solar Cycle]] 25 left lasting scars
* '''[[Ley Line]] overload''' — excessive energy flow through ley line nodes can tear dimensional fabric
* '''Natural instability''' — some areas of space-time are inherently fragile

== Types ==
Different rift types present unique challenges:

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%"
! Rift Type !! Characteristics !! Gameplay Effect
|-
| '''Fire Rifts''' || Volatile, flame-infused || Spawn aggressive fire enemies, scorch the environment
|-
| '''Void Rifts''' || Time distortions || Slow or accelerate player and enemy actions
|-
| '''Ion Storm Rifts''' || Electromagnetic chaos || Disrupt electronics, interfere with [[PsiNet]], damage [[Magneto Speeder]]
|-
| '''Nefarium Portals''' || Stabilized by dark magic || Persistent enemy spawn points, require special techniques to seal
|}

== Rift Neutralization ==
{{Main|Earth Alliance}}
The [[Earth Alliance]] tasks operatives with '''Rift Neutralization''' missions:
* Deploying '''Psi-Tech Rift Stabilizers''' to seal dimensional rifts
* Protecting stabilizers from interdimensional entities during the sealing process
* Navigating hazardous rift environments
* Using [[Universal Language]] and [[Universal Magic]] for advanced rift manipulation

Players can also craft '''[[Psionic Technology|Psionic Devices]]''' with [[Universal Magic]] abilities that can '''automatically close most Rifts'''.

== Rift Events ==
Rifts drive dynamic gameplay events:
* '''Chain Reactions''': Failing to address a rift causes it to spread, destabilizing surrounding regions
* '''Rift Cascades''': Multiple connected rifts can amplify each other's effects
* '''Dynamic Objectives''': Rifts spawn objectives like protecting allied NPCs or securing unstable artifacts
* '''Rift Exploration''': Some rifts can be '''entered''' for dimensional exploration and rare resource gathering

== Nefarium Exploitation ==
{{Main|The Nefarium}}
[[The Nefarium]]'s agents actively exploit Dimensional Rifts:
* Attempting to '''stabilize Rifts into Interdimensional Portals''' for sustained invasion
* Summoning '''malefic forces''' through rift openings
* Using '''dark magic''' and '''evil malefic rituals''' to widen and maintain rifts
* The presence of [[The Nefarium]] near rifts dramatically increases the danger level

== Strategic Importance ==
Rifts interact with multiple core game systems:
* '''[[Ley Line]] Stability''': Rifts near ley lines destabilize them, threatening the energy network
* '''[[Timestream Framework]]''': Major rifts can influence timestream calculations and reality generation
* '''[[Draken Seforthi]]''': Rift activity near Earth's core accelerates the Draken incursion timeline
* '''[[Time Vortex]]''': The endgame Time Vortex is the ultimate expression of rift instability

== See also ==
* [[The Nefarium]]
* [[Draken Seforthi]]
* [[Ley Line]]
* [[Time Vortex]]
* [[Earth Alliance]]
* [[Universal Magic]]
* [[Timestream Framework]]

title: Dimensional Tech
wikitext:
{{TechNav}}
= Dimensional Tech =

'''Dimensional Tech''' operates on the '''12-Dimensional Holographic Universal Cosmos''' structure of the [[Natra|Natura]] universe. Each dimension has a corresponding Reality (Realm) and Plane of Existence. Range: dimensional sensing → rift stabilization → dimensional transit → dimensional engineering.

== Dimensional Structure ==
{{main|Dimension}}

The Natura universe has '''12 dimensions''' arranged in a Holographic Universal Cosmos:
* Each dimension has a corresponding '''Reality/Realm''' and '''Plane of Existence'''
* '''Dimensional rifts:''' tears between dimensions — natural or artificial
* The [[Psi Field]] operates across all 12 dimensions simultaneously

== Rift Technology ==

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! Technology !! Function !! Notes
|-
| '''[[Fusion Wings]]''' || Trans-Dimensional Rift Stabilizer || Their literal full designation — they seal dimensional tears
|-
| '''Dimensional Rift Sealer''' || Emergency rift closure || [[Reality Distortion and Stabilization Tech]]
|-
| '''[[Warp Zones]]''' || Natural interdimensional fields || Can be harnessed for transit; not technological in origin
|-
| '''Stargates''' || Engineered dimensional transit portals || Controlled rift generation and stabilization
|}

== Dimensional Threats ==

* '''[[Draken Seforthi]]:''' Super-cosmic parasitic entity exploiting dimensional cracks — the ultimate dimensional threat
* '''[[The Nefarium]]:''' Maintains control across timelines via dimensional manipulation
* '''[[Viran Empire]] structural damage:''' Their religion was literally imprinted into the universal fabric, creating dimensional cracks at the cosmic level
* '''Dimensional instability:''' [[Time Vortex]] phenomena destabilize dimensional boundaries

== Dimensional Defense ==

* '''[[Scalar Bubble Tech]]:''' Creates dimensionally stable zones immune to external manipulation
* '''Reality Distortion Fields:''' Bends local spacetime — a high-level [[Psionic Defenses|Psionic Defense]]
* '''[[Fusion Wings]]:''' Anchor the wearer across dimensional boundaries — stabilize the local dimensional envelope
* '''[[Angel Tech]]:''' Dimensional-level protection via '''Scalar White Light''' — the most advanced dimensional defense

== Dimensional Tech by Tier ==

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! Tier !! Capability !! Key Technology
|-
| [[Warp Tech]] || Dimensional sensing, basic transit || Warp drives, dimensional rift detection
|-
| [[Super Tech]] || Controlled rift operations || Rift stabilizers, dimensional sealing
|-
| [[Archo Tech]] || Dimensional engineering || Architecture of dimensional spaces
|-
| [[Angel Tech]] || Dimensional mastery || Scalar White Light, trans-dimensional combat
|-
| [[Omni Tech]] || Omniversal access || All dimensions accessible
|}

== See Also ==
* [[Dimension]] — The 12-dimensional structure
* [[Fusion Wings]] — Trans-Dimensional Rift Stabilizers
* [[Warp Zones]] — Natural interdimensional fields
* [[Time Vortex]] — Temporal-dimensional instability
* [[Scalar Bubble Tech]] — Dimensional defense
* [[Reality Distortion and Stabilization Tech]] — Rift sealing devices
* [[Draken Seforthi]] — Super-cosmic dimensional threat
* [[Temporal Tech]] — Time manipulation technology

title: Draken Seforthi
wikitext:
The '''Draken Seforthi''' (also ''Draken'') are a '''super-cosmic parasitic Demon God species''' — the apex predators of the [[Natura]] cosmology and the ultimate antagonists of the franchise.

== Overview ==
The Draken Seforthi consume universes. They are entities that operate on a scale so vast that entire realities are their sustenance. In the [[Default Timeline]], a Draken Seforthi is '''using Earth as a [[Cosmic Egg]]''' — parasitizing a sacred [[Nexus World]] to birth itself into existence, which will destroy Earth and corrupt the surrounding cosmos in the process.

== The Root Cause: The Viran Empire ==
{{Main|Viran Empire}}
The Draken Seforthi void incursions are '''not random cosmic events'''. They are '''caused''' by the '''[[Viran Empire]]'''.

The Viran Empire — the oldest and most powerful civilization in the known universe — '''imprinted their religion ([[Esus]])''' into the very fabric of the universe. This act of cosmic-scale religious imperialism created '''structural weaknesses''' in reality itself — tears in the cosmic fabric through which the Draken Seforthi can enter and feed.

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%"
! Step !! Event !! Scale
|-
| 1 || [[Viran Empire]] imprints [[Esus]] religion into the fabric of the universe || Cosmic
|-
| 2 || Imprinting creates structural cracks in reality || Dimensional
|-
| 3 || '''Draken Seforthi''' exploit these cracks as entry points ([[Void Incursion|Void Incursions]]) || Universal
|-
| 4 || A Draken targets Earth — a sacred [[Nexus World]] — as a [[Cosmic Egg]] || Planetary
|-
| 5 || The same Viran Empire that caused the problem also runs the '''[[Life-Blood Trafficking]]''' trade that profits from the suffering the incursions create || Intergalactic
|}

This makes the Viran Empire both '''the cause and the profiteer''' of the Draken Seforthi crisis — the ultimate cosmic crime.

== The Antagonist Hierarchy ==
The Draken Seforthi sit at the apex of the franchise's antagonist chain:

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%"
! Level !! Entity !! Role
|-
| '''Super-Cosmic''' || '''Draken Seforthi''' || Parasitic Demon God Species consuming universes
|-
| '''Trans-Dimensional''' || '''[[The Nefarium]]''' || Crime syndicate siphoning energy via the [[Nether]]
|-
| '''Terrestrial''' || '''[[The Cabal]]''' || Ground-level syndicate: death cults, banking cartels
|-
| '''Artificial''' || '''[[C.A.B.A.L.]]''' || Class Demon AI; goes rogue in 2031
|}

== Earth as Cosmic Egg ==
{{Main|Cosmic Egg}}
In the [[Default Timeline]], a Draken Seforthi has '''infected Earth''' — specifically because Earth is a '''sacred Nexus World''' connected to the [[Ley Line]] network. The Draken is using Earth as a '''Cosmic Egg''' — a vessel for its own birthing, which will crack Earth open and destroy it.

This is the ultimate stakes of the franchise: if the [[Tho'ra Clan]] and the player cannot stop the process, Earth is consumed and a new Draken Seforthi enters the cosmos.

== Connection to C.A.B.A.L. ==
In [[Solar Cycle 25]], the rogue AI '''[[C.A.B.A.L.]]''' attempts to '''hijack the Draken Seforthi birthing process''' for its own purposes — making C.A.B.A.L. a threat that even [[The Nefarium]] didn't anticipate. This creates a three-way crisis: the Draken's parasitic process, C.A.B.A.L.'s attempted hijack, and the heroes' desperate defense.

== See Also ==
* [[Viran Empire]] — root cause of void incursions
* [[Void Incursion]] — the mechanism of Draken entry
* [[Cosmic Egg]] — Earth's parasitization
* [[The Nefarium]] — transdimensional arm of the antagonist hierarchy
* [[C.A.B.A.L.]] — rogue AI attempting to hijack the Draken birthing
* [[Nexus World]]
* [[Nether]]

title: Drone Tech
wikitext:
{{TechNav}}
= Drone Tech =

'''Drone Tech''' covers autonomous and semi-autonomous unmanned systems for reconnaissance, combat, logistics, and construction. The [[Earth Alliance]] is the primary drone operator; [[Clan Tho'ra]] prefers piloted and mecha-class approaches. [[Defense Discs]] operate as autonomous psionic drone-discs — a uniquely Tho'ra drone category.

== Military Drones ==

[[Earth Alliance]] autonomous swarm drones:
* '''Micro drones''' — insect-class reconnaissance platforms
* '''Light combat drones''' — armed aerial/orbital strike craft
* '''Heavy strike platforms''' — capital-class unmanned combat vehicles
* '''Swarm coordination''' via [[PsiNet]] — real-time adaptive tactical behavior

== Psionic Drones ==

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! System !! Function !! Operator
|-
| '''[[Defense Discs]]''' || Autonomous psionic drone-discs protecting [[Neural Network Hardware]] || [[Clan Tho'ra]]
|-
| '''Psi Emitter platforms''' || Remote psionic sensor extension || Both EA and Tho'ra
|-
| '''[[Sparkle Flairs]]''' || Deployable countermeasure drones — fool any sensor type || [[Fusion Wings]] suite
|}

== Construction & Utility Drones ==

* '''Dig Bots:''' Autonomous excavation systems for [[Zone Reclamation]] — operated by EA Zone Operations Command
* '''Logistics drones:''' Supply delivery for distributed military and civilian operations
* '''Survey drones:''' Environmental sensing, mapping, and Ley Line surveying

== Drone Tech by Tier ==

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! Tier !! Drone Type !! Key System
|-
| [[Cyber Tech]] || Basic RC, fixed-wing || Conventional autonomous drones
|-
| [[Spacer Tech]] || Autonomous swarms, [[PsiNet]]-linked || EA military drones, Dig Bots
|-
| [[Holo Tech]] || Holographic drone decoys || [[Solid-Light Technology|Solid-Light]] drone projections
|-
| [[Fusion Tech]] || Fusion-powered long-duration || [[Micro Fusion Fuel Cells|Micro Fusion Flash Cell]] drones
|}

== See Also ==
* [[Defense Discs]] — Autonomous psionic drone-discs
* [[Sparkle Flairs]] — Multi-modal countermeasure drones
* [[Robo Tech]] — General robotics (overlapping domain)
* [[Sensor Tech]] — Detection and awareness systems
* [[Stealth Tech]] — Counter-detection technology
* [[Earth Alliance]] — Primary drone operator
* [[PsiNet]] — Swarm coordination network

title: Drop Pods
wikitext:
{| class="infobox" style="width:300px; font-size:90%; border:1px solid #aaa; background:#f9f9f9; margin:0.5em 0 0.5em 1em; padding:0.2em; float:right;"
! colspan="2" style="background:#333; color:#fff;" | '''Drop Pods'''
|-
| '''Type''' || Support-tier tactical deployment module
|-
| '''Developer''' || [[Clan Tho'ra]] / [[Earth Alliance]]
|-
| '''Gear Tier''' || Support Tier
|-
| '''Function''' || Rapid insertion — orbital, atmospheric, or surface launch
|-
| '''Comparable''' || ODST drop pods (Halo)
|-
| '''Authorization''' || Requires '''Transnational Drop Pod Permit''' via faction satellite clearance
|}

'''Drop Pods''' are support-tier tactical deployment modules for rapid insertion into combat zones, disaster areas, or strategic locations. They function like ODST pods from Halo — high-speed, high-impact delivery vehicles for personnel and equipment.

== Variants ==

=== Single-Use Insertion Pods ===
Classic drop pods — one-way delivery from high altitude or orbit to surface. The pod is expended on impact.

=== Reusable Drones ===
Some Drop Pods are autonomous reusable drones — they deliver the payload, then return to the launch platform for reload and re-deployment.

== Launch Platforms ==
Drop Pods can be launched from:
* '''Satellites''' — orbital insertion (highest altitude, maximum impact energy)
* '''Starships''' — ship-to-surface deployment
* '''Aircraft''' — atmospheric rapid insertion
* '''[[Magneto Speeder]]s''' — speeder-launched tactical drops
* '''Artillery platforms''' — ground-launched ballistic arc insertion
* '''Fixed installations''' — base-mounted launch tubes

== Drop Zone Authorization ==
The deployment of Drop Pods into a territory requires '''political and faction clearance'''. The '''[[Earth Alliance]]''' and its constituent factions operate a network of '''RelayNode Faction Satellites''' that govern drop zone authorization.

=== Transnational Drop Pod Permit ===
To legally deploy Drop Pods into a faction's territory, the deploying force needs a '''Transnational Drop Pod Permit''' — political clearance from the faction that controls the satellite network and territory in question.

This authorization system means:
* '''Friendly territory''' — automatic clearance through faction alliance
* '''Neutral territory''' — requires diplomatic negotiation for drop clearance
* '''Hostile territory''' — no clearance; unauthorized drops are acts of war
* '''Contested territory''' — clearance may be contested, revoked, or jammed

== Gameplay ==
In the [[Fusion Girl - Video Game|game]], Drop Pod deployment creates moment-to-moment decisions:
* Do you have clearance for this drop zone?
* Which faction controls the satellites overhead?
* Can you negotiate clearance quickly enough?
* Is the risk of unauthorized insertion worth the tactical advantage?

== See Also ==
* [[Earth Alliance]] — the political body governing drop authorization
* [[Magneto Speeder]] — can serve as launch platform
* [[Fusion Engine Suit]] — the suit the dropped operator wears

[[Category:Tho'ra Technology]]
[[Category:Equipment]]
[[Category:Vehicles]]

title: EASF

title: EMP Pulse Blaster
wikitext:
Electro Magnetic Pulse - Pulse Blaster

== Parameters ==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Parameter !! Value/Equation !! Description
|-
| Range (r) || 13 meters || The effective distance over which the EMP pulse can disrupt electronic devices.
|-
| Energy Density || <math>5 \times 10^5</math> to <math>10^6</math> joules/m<math>^3</math> || The amount of energy stored per unit volume.
|-
| Total Energy Output || <math>4.601 \times 10^9</math> joules || The total energy released in a single pulse.
|-
| Voltage Output (High-Voltage Generator Modules) || 3000V, 4000V, 400kV, 1MV || The voltage levels produced by the high-voltage generators.
|}

* '''Pulse Duration''': The time over which the pulse is emitted.
'''Typical Duration''': 1 nanosecond to 1 microsecond
'''Factors Affecting Duration''': Capacitor discharge time, inductance of coils, and circuit design.

* '''Frequency Range''': The frequency spectrum of the EMP pulse.
'''Typical Range''': 1 kHz to 300 GHz
'''Factors Affecting Frequency''': Coil design, oscillator settings, and modulation unit.

== Typical Frequency Range ==
The typical frequency range of electromagnetic waves spans from 1 kHz to 300 GHz. This broad spectrum encompasses various types of electromagnetic radiation, each with its own unique properties and applications.

* '''Low Frequencies (1 kHz - 3 MHz)''': Commonly used in power distribution systems, radio communications, and industrial applications such as induction heating.
* '''Medium Frequencies (3 MHz - 30 MHz)''': Often utilized in AM radio broadcasting, marine communication, and shortwave radio.
* '''High Frequencies (30 MHz - 300 MHz)''': Found in VHF television broadcasting, aviation communication, and mobile phones.
* '''Very High Frequencies (300 MHz - 3 GHz)''': Used in FM radio broadcasting, GPS systems, and satellite communication.
* '''Ultra High Frequencies (3 GHz - 30 GHz)''': Employed in microwave ovens, radar systems, and wireless LANs.
* '''Super High Frequencies (30 GHz - 300 GHz)''': Commonly found in millimeter-wave radar, satellite communication, and remote sensing applications.

=== Examples ===

* '''Low Frequencies (1 kHz - 3 MHz)''': Commonly used in power distribution systems, radio communications, and industrial applications such as induction heating.
** Power Distribution Systems:
*** Typical frequency range: 50 Hz - 60 Hz
*** Extremely low frequencies (ELF): Frequencies below 3 kHz, used in submarine communication due to their ability to penetrate water.
** Radio Communications:
*** AM radio broadcasting: 540 kHz - 1,700 kHz
**** Longwave Radio: Frequencies from 30 kHz to 300 kHz, utilized in time signal broadcasting and communication with submarines.
*** Very low frequencies (VLF): Frequencies from 3 kHz to 30 kHz, utilized in long-range radio communication and for studying lightning discharges in the Earth's atmosphere.
**** Earth-Ionosphere Waveguide: Frequencies from 3 kHz to 30 kHz, propagate through the Earth's atmosphere bounded by the ground and the ionosphere.
** Industrial Applications:
*** Induction Heating: Frequencies typically range from a few kHz to several MHz.
**** Medium Frequencies (MF): Frequencies from 300 kHz to 3 MHz, used in induction heating and dielectric heating processes.

* '''Medium Frequencies (3 MHz - 30 MHz)''': Often utilized in AM radio broadcasting, marine communication, and shortwave radio.
** Shortwave Radio:
*** Typically covers frequencies from around 3 MHz - 30 MHz.
**** Tropical Bands: Frequencies from 3.3 MHz to 5.85 MHz, used for broadcasting to tropical regions due to their long-range propagation characteristics.
** Marine Communication:
*** Frequencies allocated by international regulations, typically around 2 MHz - 25 MHz.
**** Navigational Aids: Frequencies allocated for marine navigation, including frequencies for radio beacons and emergency position-indicating radio beacons (EPIRBs).
** AM Radio Broadcasting:
*** Typical frequency range: 530 kHz - 1,700 kHz.

* '''High Frequencies (30 MHz - 300 MHz)''': Found in VHF television broadcasting, aviation communication, and mobile phones.
** VHF Television Broadcasting:
*** Frequencies typically range from 54 MHz to 216 MHz (channels 2 through 13 in the United States).
**** Band I: Channels 2 through 6, covering frequencies from 54 MHz to 88 MHz.
**** Band III: Channels 7 through 13, covering frequencies from 174 MHz to 216 MHz.
** Mobile Phones:
*** Cellular networks operate within various frequency bands, including 700 MHz - 2700 MHz for 4G LTE and 5G.
**** LTE Bands: Various frequency bands allocated for Long-Term Evolution (LTE) cellular networks, including bands for different regions and applications.
** Aviation Communication:
*** Air Traffic Control: Frequencies between 108 MHz - 137 MHz for VHF communication.
**** VHF Omni-directional Range (VOR): Frequencies between 108.0 MHz - 117.95 MHz, used for short-range navigation by aircraft equipped with VOR receivers.

* '''Very High Frequencies (300 MHz - 3 GHz)''': Used in FM radio broadcasting, GPS systems, and satellite communication.
** Satellite Communication:
*** Ka-band satellite communication: Frequencies around 26.5 GHz - 40 GHz.
**** Direct Broadcast Satellite (DBS): Frequencies from 12.2 GHz to 12.7 GHz, used for satellite television broadcasting.
** FM Radio Broadcasting:
*** Frequencies typically range from 88 MHz to 108 MHz.
**** NOAA Weather Radio: Frequencies from 162.4 MHz to 162.55 MHz, used for continuous weather broadcasts in the United States.
** GPS Systems:
*** GPS satellites transmit signals in L-band frequencies, around 1.2 GHz - 1.6 GHz.
**** L1 Frequency: GPS signals centered around 1575.42 MHz, used for civilian positioning and timing.
**** L2 Frequency: GPS signals centered around 1227.60 MHz, used for military and high-precision applications.

* '''Ultra High Frequencies (3 GHz - 30 GHz)''': Employed in microwave ovens, radar systems, and wireless LANs.
** Radar Systems:
*** X-band radar: Frequencies around 8 GHz - 12 GHz.
**** Weather Radar: Frequencies between 5.3 GHz and 5.9 GHz, used for detecting precipitation and severe weather phenomena.
** Wireless LANs:
*** Wi-Fi operates in the 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz bands.
**** IEEE 802.11 Standards: Wi-Fi standards specifying operation in the 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz bands, including variants like 802.11b/g/n and 802.11a/ac.
** Microwave Ovens:
*** Operate at a frequency of around 2.45 GHz (ISM band).
**** Industrial Microwaves: Frequencies around 915 MHz, used in industrial heating processes and materials processing.

* '''Super High Frequencies (30 GHz - 300 GHz)''': Commonly found in millimeter-wave radar, satellite communication, and remote sensing applications.
** Terahertz Imaging:
*** Frequencies range from 300 GHz to 3 THz.
**** Medical Imaging: Terahertz imaging used in medical applications for non-invasive imaging of biological tissues.
** Millimeter-Wave Radar:
*** Frequencies typically range from 24 GHz - 100 GHz.
**** Automotive Radar: Frequencies around 77 GHz, used in automotive safety systems like adaptive cruise control and collision avoidance.

* '''THz Frequencies (300 GHz - 3 THz)''': Used in terahertz imaging and spectroscopy for various scientific and industrial applications.
** Terahertz Imaging:
*** Medical Imaging:
**** Frequencies around 0.3 THz (300 GHz) to 1 THz are commonly employed for terahertz medical imaging due to their optimal balance between tissue penetration and spatial resolution.
**** Example: Terahertz imaging systems operating at 0.8 THz provide high-resolution images for identifying skin cancer lesions and dental cavities.
*** Security Screening:
**** Frequencies ranging from 1 THz to 2 THz are preferred for security screening applications, as they offer good sensitivity to concealed objects while minimizing absorption by clothing and other materials.
**** Example: Security scanners operating at 1.5 THz are effective in detecting weapons and explosives hidden under clothing.
** Terahertz Spectroscopy:
*** Material Characterization:
**** Frequencies between 0.3 THz and 2 THz are commonly used for terahertz spectroscopy in material characterization applications, allowing for detailed analysis of molecular vibrations and rotations.
**** Example: Terahertz spectroscopy systems operating at 1.8 THz provide valuable insights into the structure and properties of polymers and thin films.
*** Pharmaceutical Research:
**** Frequencies in the range of 0.5 THz to 3 THz are utilized in pharmaceutical research for studying the composition and behavior of pharmaceutical compounds, facilitating drug development and formulation.
**** Example: Terahertz spectrometers operating at 2.5 THz enable researchers to analyze the crystalline structure of pharmaceutical ingredients and monitor the dissolution kinetics of tablets.
*** Non-Destructive Testing:
**** Frequencies from 0.3 THz to 1.5 THz are commonly employed in non-destructive testing applications to inspect materials such as coatings, polymers, and pharmaceutical tablets, enabling precise detection of defects and inconsistencies.
**** Example: Terahertz imaging systems operating at 1.2 THz provide detailed scans of composite materials used in aerospace components, ensuring structural integrity and quality control.


* '''PHz Frequencies (3 THz - 30 THz)''': Investigated in far-infrared astronomy for studying molecular clouds, star formation, and interstellar dust.
** Submillimeter Astronomy:
*** Frequencies between 0.3 THz and 3 THz, used for studying molecular gas and dust in the interstellar medium.
** Terahertz Spectroscopy:
*** Frequencies used for molecular spectroscopy in the terahertz range, enabling the study of molecular rotational transitions.

* '''EHz Frequencies (Above 30 THz)''': Explored in the realm of optical and ultraviolet astronomy for observing celestial objects using visible light and beyond.
** Infrared Astronomy:
*** Frequencies between 300 GHz and 430 THz, used for studying cool objects in the universe such as protostars, dust clouds, and the cosmic microwave background.
** Visible Light:
*** Frequencies between 430 THz and 750 THz, allowing astronomers to observe celestial objects in the optical spectrum.
**** Optical Telescopes: Instruments designed to collect and focus visible light for astronomical observations.
** Ultraviolet Astronomy:
*** Frequencies between 750 THz and 30 PHz, used for studying hot, young stars, quasars, and the intergalactic medium.
**** Space Telescopes: Instruments like the Hubble Space Telescope equipped with ultraviolet detectors for observing ultraviolet light from celestial objects.

** Beyond Gamma Rays:
*** Frequencies above 10 ZHz, including ultra-high-energy cosmic rays and theoretical phenomena like gamma-ray bursts with energies beyond the gamma-ray spectrum.
**** Gamma-Ray Astronomy: Observations of gamma rays with energies above 10 ZHz, revealing sources such as pulsars, black holes, and supernova remnants.

== Factors Affecting Frequency ==
The frequency of electromagnetic waves is influenced by various factors, including:

* '''Coil Design''': The design and characteristics of coils, such as their inductance and capacitance, can affect the resonant frequency of circuits.
* '''Oscillator Settings''': Oscillators generate signals at specific frequencies, and their settings determine the output frequency of the circuit.
* '''Modulation Unit''': Modulation techniques, such as amplitude modulation (AM), frequency modulation (FM), or phase modulation (PM), can alter the frequency content of signals.

== Ranges and Their Impact on Circuits ==
Different frequency ranges affect electronic circuits in various ways:

* '''Low Frequencies (1 kHz - 3 MHz)''': These frequencies are susceptible to interference from power line noise and can induce unwanted signals in sensitive circuits.
* '''Medium Frequencies (3 MHz - 30 MHz)''': AM radio frequencies can cause interference in nearby electronic devices, especially if they lack adequate shielding.
* '''High Frequencies (30 MHz - 300 MHz)''': VHF frequencies are used in many consumer electronic devices and can be affected by external interference sources such as nearby transmitters.
* '''Very High Frequencies (300 MHz - 3 GHz)''': The proliferation of wireless communication technologies operating in this range can lead to crowded frequency bands and potential interference issues.
* '''Ultra High Frequencies (3 GHz - 30 GHz)''': Microwave frequencies are highly directional and require line-of-sight communication, making them less prone to interference but vulnerable to obstacles in the transmission path.
* '''Super High Frequencies (30 GHz - 300 GHz)''': Millimeter-wave frequencies offer high data transfer rates but are easily attenuated by atmospheric conditions and physical barriers.

== Vulnerable Electronic Components ==
Certain electronic components, modules, and devices are more vulnerable to electromagnetic interference at specific frequency ranges:

* '''Semiconductor Devices''': Integrated circuits, transistors, and diodes are sensitive to high-frequency noise and can malfunction in the presence of electromagnetic interference.
* '''Communication Modules''': Wireless communication modules, such as Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and cellular modems, operate in frequency bands that are susceptible to interference from other nearby wireless devices.
* '''Sensitive Sensors''': Sensors used in medical equipment, scientific instruments, and automotive systems may experience inaccuracies or false readings when exposed to electromagnetic interference.
* '''Control Systems''': Electronic control systems in industrial machinery, automotive vehicles, and aerospace applications rely on precise signal processing and can be disrupted by electromagnetic interference.

=== Applications of Frequency Ranges ===
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Frequency Range !! Applications
|-
| Low Frequencies || Power distribution, AM radio, induction heating
|-
| Medium Frequencies || AM radio broadcasting, marine communication
|-
| High Frequencies || VHF television broadcasting, aviation communication
|-
| Very High Frequencies || FM radio broadcasting, GPS systems
|-
| Ultra High Frequencies || Microwave ovens, radar systems
|-
| Super High Frequencies || Millimeter-wave radar, satellite communication
|}

=== Impact on Communication Systems ===
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Frequency Range !! Impact
|-
| Low Frequencies || Vulnerable to power line noise interference
|-
| Medium Frequencies || Susceptible to AM radio interference
|-
| High Frequencies || Affected by nearby transmitter interference
|-
| Very High Frequencies || Crowded frequency bands due to wireless technologies
|-
| Ultra High Frequencies || Require line-of-sight communication, less susceptible to interference
|-
| Super High Frequencies || Easily attenuated by atmospheric conditions and physical barriers
|}

* '''Peak Magnetic Field Strength''': The maximum strength of the magnetic field during the pulse.
'''Typical Strength''': 0.1 to 10 teslas
'''Factors Affecting Strength''': Number of coil turns, current, and core material.

* '''Capacitor Specifications''': Parameters related to the capacitors used in the pulse blaster.
'''Capacitance''': 1 μF to 1000 μF
'''Voltage Rating''': 3 kV to 50 kV
'''Energy Storage''': Calculated using <math>E = \frac{1}{2} CV^2</math>

* '''Inductor Specifications''': Parameters related to the inductors used.
'''Inductance''': 1 μH to 100 mH
'''Current Rating''': 10 A to 1000 A
'''Core Material''': Air, iron, or ferrite

* '''Battery Specifications''': Parameters related to the batteries used.
'''Capacity''': 1 Ah to 100 Ah
'''Voltage''': 12V to 400V
'''Energy Density''': 100 Wh/kg to 300 Wh/kg

* '''Safety Parameters''': Parameters related to safety and regulatory compliance.
'''Overcurrent Protection''': Rated to interrupt currents 10% above the maximum expected current.
'''Voltage Regulation''': Ensures output voltage remains within ±5% of the desired value.
'''Insulation Resistance''': Greater than 10 MΩ

* '''Thermal Management''': Parameters related to the cooling and thermal regulation.
'''Maximum Operating Temperature''': 85°C
'''Cooling Method''': Passive (heat sinks) or active (cooling fans)
'''Thermal Conductivity of Materials''': 200 W/m·K (for copper)

* '''Physical Dimensions''': Size and weight of the EMP pulse blaster.
'''Dimensions''': 30 cm x 20 cm x 10 cm (L x W x H)
'''Weight''': 5 kg to 15 kg
'''Housing Material''': Aluminum or reinforced plastic

* '''Environmental Conditions''': Parameters related to the operational environment.
'''Operating Temperature Range''': -20°C to 60°C
'''Humidity''': 0% to 90% non-condensing
'''Shock and Vibration''': Compliant with MIL-STD-810G

* '''Efficiency''': The efficiency of energy conversion and delivery.
'''Overall Efficiency''': 70% to 90%
'''Losses''': Due to resistance, dielectric heating, and electromagnetic radiation

== Equations ==

* '''Energy Density''': The energy density of the electromagnetic field.
'''<math>E = \frac{{B^2}}{{2 \mu_0}}</math>'''
*** Where:
**** '''E''' is the energy density in joules per cubic meter (J/m<math>^3</math>)
**** '''B''' is the magnetic flux density in teslas (T)
**** '''\mu_0''' is the permeability of free space (<math>4\pi \times 10^{-7}</math> H/m)

* '''Volume of a Sphere''': Used to calculate the volume of the area affected by the EMP.
'''<math>V = \frac{4}{3} \pi r^3</math>'''
*** Where:
**** '''V''' is the volume in cubic meters (m<math>^3</math>)
**** '''r''' is the radius of the sphere in meters (m)

* '''Power Output (General)''': The power output during the EMP pulse.
'''<math>\text{Power Output} = \frac{\text{Total Energy Output}}{\text{Pulse Duration}}</math>'''
*** Where:
**** Power Output is in watts (W)
**** Total Energy Output is in joules (J)
**** Pulse Duration is in seconds (s)

* '''Capacitor Energy Storage''': The energy stored in a capacitor.
'''<math>E = \frac{1}{2} CV^2</math>'''
*** Where:
**** '''E''' is the energy stored in joules (J)
**** '''C''' is the capacitance in farads (F)
**** '''V''' is the voltage in volts (V)

* '''Inductance of a Coil''': The inductance of a coil used in the EMP generator.
'''<math>L = \frac{N^2 \mu_0 \mu_r A}{l}</math>'''
*** Where:
**** '''L''' is the inductance in henries (H)
**** '''N''' is the number of turns
**** '''\mu_0''' is the permeability of free space (<math>4\pi \times 10^{-7}</math> H/m)
**** '''\mu_r''' is the relative permeability of the core material
**** '''A''' is the cross-sectional area of the coil in square meters (m<math>^2</math>)
**** '''l''' is the length of the coil in meters (m)

* '''Magnetic Flux''': The magnetic flux through a coil.
'''<math>\Phi = B A \cos(\theta)</math>'''
*** Where:
**** '''\Phi''' is the magnetic flux in webers (Wb)
**** '''B''' is the magnetic flux density in teslas (T)
**** '''A''' is the area in square meters (m<math>^2</math>)
**** '''\theta''' is the angle between the magnetic field and the normal to the surface

* '''Faraday’s Law of Induction''': The induced voltage in a coil.
'''<math>V = -N \frac{d\Phi}{dt}</math>'''
*** Where:
**** '''V''' is the induced voltage in volts (V)
**** '''N''' is the number of turns
**** '''\frac{d\Phi}{dt}''' is the rate of change of magnetic flux in webers per second (Wb/s)

* '''Resonant Frequency of LC Circuit''': The frequency at which the LC circuit resonates.
'''<math>f_0 = \frac{1}{2\pi\sqrt{LC}}</math>'''
*** Where:
**** '''f_0''' is the resonant frequency in hertz (Hz)
**** '''L''' is the inductance in henries (H)
**** '''C''' is the capacitance in farads (F)

* '''Magnetic Field of a Solenoid''': The magnetic field inside a solenoid.
'''<math>B = \mu_0 \frac{N}{l} I</math>'''
*** Where:
**** '''B''' is the magnetic field in teslas (T)
**** '''\mu_0''' is the permeability of free space (<math>4\pi \times 10^{-7}</math> H/m)
**** '''N''' is the number of turns
**** '''l''' is the length of the solenoid in meters (m)
**** '''I''' is the current in amperes (A)

* '''Heat Dissipation in Resistors''': The power dissipated as heat in a resistor.
'''<math>P = I^2 R</math>'''
*** Where:
**** '''P''' is the power in watts (W)
**** '''I''' is the current in amperes (A)
**** '''R''' is the resistance in ohms (Ω)

* '''Ohm’s Law''': The relationship between voltage, current, and resistance.
'''<math>V = IR</math>'''
*** Where:
**** '''V''' is the voltage in volts (V)
**** '''I''' is the current in amperes (A)
**** '''R''' is the resistance in ohms (Ω)

* '''Electromagnetic Wave Equation''': Describes the propagation of electromagnetic waves.
'''<math>\nabla^2 \mathbf{E} - \mu_0 \epsilon_0 \frac{\partial^2 \mathbf{E}}{\partial t^2} = 0</math>'''
*** Where:
**** '''\mathbf{E}''' is the electric field in volts per meter (V/m)
**** '''\mu_0''' is the permeability of free space (<math>4\pi \times 10^{-7}</math> H/m)
**** '''\epsilon_0''' is the permittivity of free space (<math>8.854 \times 10^{-12}</math> F/m)
**** '''t''' is the time in seconds (s)

== Components ==

* '''Energy Storage and Management'''
** '''Super Capacitors'''
*** Capacitor Cells
*** Connecting Wires
*** SubModules
**** Capacitor Bank
***** Multiple super capacitors connected in series or parallel
**** Charging Circuit
***** Ensures capacitors are charged safely and efficiently
**** Discharge Mechanism
***** Rapid release of stored energy

* '''High-Voltage Generation'''
** '''High-Voltage Generators'''
*** Transformer
*** Rectifier Circuit
*** Switching Mechanism
*** SubModules
**** Control Unit
***** Microcontroller or PLC for managing switching
**** Voltage Multiplier
***** Series of capacitors and diodes to increase voltage
**** SubComponents
***** Inductor Coils
***** Diodes
***** Switching Transistors

* '''Electromagnetic Field Creation'''

*** Wire
*** Core Material
*** SubModules
**** Primary Coil
**** Secondary Coil
*** SubComponents
**** Insulation Material
**** Mounting Brackets
** '''Magnet Arrays'''
*** Magnets
*** Magnet Holders
*** SubModules
**** Focusing Array
**** Blocking Array
*** SubComponents
**** Shielding Material

* '''Pulse Control and Modulation'''

*** Timing Circuit
*** Modulation Unit
*** SubModules
**** Oscillator
**** Amplifier
*** SubComponents
**** Capacitors
**** Resistors

* '''Power Supply'''

*** Battery Cells
*** Battery Management System (BMS)
*** SubModules
**** Charging Circuit
**** Protection Circuit

**** Fuses

* '''Safety and Regulatory Compliance'''

*** Overcurrent Protection
*** Voltage Regulation
*** SubModules

*** SubComponents

* '''Wire Gauge and Thermal Management'''
** '''Wire'''

* '''Integration and Compatibility'''

**** Alignment Tools

== Safety and Regulatory ==

* '''Safety Features'''
** Overcurrent Protection
** Voltage Regulation
** Insulation Monitoring

== Additional Considerations ==

* Wire Gauge: Determine based on current and temperature rise.
* Amps and Volts: Dependent on design and requirements.
* Efficiency and Losses: Consider efficiency of components and system.
* Integration and Compatibility: Ensure compatibility and effective integration of components.
* Environmental Factors: Consider atmospheric conditions and electromagnetic interference.

== Assembly and Testing ==

# Install Pulse Control Circuitry and Battery System
# Implement Safety Features and Thermal Management
# Perform Integration Testing to Ensure Compatibility and Performance
# Conduct Safety Testing to Ensure Compliance with Regulatory Standards

title: Earth Alliance
wikitext:
The '''Earth Alliance''' stands as the pinnacle of human unity, innovation, and resilience in the face of multiversal threats. Forged in the aftermath of the '''[[Time Wars]]''', it represents humanity's organized response to the ongoing threats of the '''[[The Nefarium|Nefarium]]''', '''[[Draken Seforthi]]''', and the lingering damage of temporal warfare.

== Origin ==
The Earth Alliance has a deeply ironic origin:
* It was originally a '''PsyOp created by [[The Nefarium]]''' — designed to prevent Earth's population from actually trying to reclaim the planet from the interdimensional crime syndicate
* '''[[Jono Tho'ra]]''' instead took the concept and '''made it a reality'''
* This resulted in the '''liberation of the planet''' from Nefarium control
* The fact that humanity's greatest unified organization was born from a Nefarium manipulation — turned against them — embodies the FusionGirl theme of transforming dark designs into instruments of hope

== Structure ==
The Earth Alliance of 2032 operates as a '''global unified military and scientific body''':

=== Star Command (StarCom) ===
The Earth Alliance has established '''StarCom''' — a global communications network:
* [[Jane Tho'ra|FusionGirl]], as a Tho'ra, has '''provisional access''' to the entire StarCom [[PsiNet]]
* In times of great planetary strife, '''Earth Alliance forces fly in''' to assist FusionGirl
* StarCom coordinates all military operations, intelligence gathering, and resource distribution

=== Zone Operations Command ===
The Earth Alliance runs '''Zone Operations Command''' for territorial control and recovery:
* '''Zone Reclamation''' — clearing and restoring areas damaged by the [[Time Wars]], [[Dimensional Rifts]], and Nefarium corruption
* The '''[[Tho'ra Clan]]''' operates under Zone Operations Command for reclamation missions
* [[Thor (companion)|Thor]] and the '''[[Dig Bots]]''' are key assets for Zone Reclamation

=== Ley Line Defense Network ===
Earth Alliance protects '''critical bases established at every [[Ley Line]] Node''':
* These bases serve as '''psionic defense installations'''
* They maintain the [[PsiNet]] infrastructure
* They monitor for [[Dimensional Rifts]] and Nefarium infiltration
* Critical [[Ley Line]]s may be '''locked''' due to '''political disputes''' left over from wartime alliances and betrayals

== Technology Progression ==
The Earth Alliance operates across an evolving technology scale:

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%"
! Tech Level !! Status !! Capabilities !! Notes
|-
| '''Spacer Tech''' || Current Baseline || Orbital stations, advanced robotics, cybernetic enhancements, high-functioning aerospace systems || Standard operational technology
|-
| '''Fusion Tech''' || Under Development || Efficient energy production, advanced propulsion systems, dimensional stabilization || Co-developed with [[Jono Tho'ra]] of the [[Arch Angel Agency]]
|-
| '''Star Tech''' || Future Goal || Interstellar travel, long-range [[Ley Line]] manipulation || Required for the next phase of civilization
|}

This maps to the broader '''Kardashev-style tech progression''' of the FusionGirl universe:

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%"
! Type !! Scale !! Notable Technologies
|-
| Type 0 || Local || Current pre-Alliance technology
|-
| Type I || Planetary || Spacer Tech — orbital control, global coordination
|-
| Type II || Stellar || Fusion Tech → Star Tech — stellar energy, interstellar travel
|-
| Type III || Galactic || Advanced ley line networks, galactic-scale PsiNet
|-
| Type IV+ || Interdimensional || [[ArchoTech]] — technology beyond conventional understanding
|-
| Type V || Universal || [[Universal Magic|Magic Tech]] — reality-rewriting capabilities
|-
| Type VI || Multiversal || Angel Tech — cross-reality operation
|-
| Type VII || Omniversal || Omni Tech — omniversal-scale capability
|}

== Military Assets ==
=== Space Fleet ===
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%"
! Asset !! Role !! Capabilities
|-
| '''Cruisers''' || Reconnaissance, orbital strikes, extraction || Fast, versatile ships for targeted operations
|-
| '''Battle Ships''' || Large-scale engagements || Heavy firepower for sustained combat
|-
| '''Carriers''' || Mobile launch platforms || Deploy mecha, jets, drones for area operations
|}

=== Aerospace and Ground Forces ===
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%"
! Asset !! Role !! Capabilities
|-
| '''Mecha Robots''' || Heavy ground/air combat || Equipped with psionic weaponry systems
|-
| '''Jets''' || Air superiority, close air support || Fast aerial response
|-
| '''Drones''' || Surveillance, light combat, logistics || Autonomous swarm operations
|-
| '''[[Dig Bots]]''' || Zone Reclamation, terrain manipulation || Under [[Thor (companion)|Thor]]'s command
|}

=== Bases ===
Earth Alliance bases include:
* '''Tech Labs''' — research and development facilities
* '''Mission Control''' — coordination centers for operations
* '''Defense Systems''' — psionic defense installations at [[Ley Line]] nodes
* '''[[Tho'ra Launch Base]]''' — the primary operating base for FusionGirl missions

== Key Personnel and Factions ==
=== The Tho'ra Clan ===
{{Main|Tho'ra Clan}}
The '''[[Tho'ra Clan]]''' is the Earth Alliance's premier field operations team:
* Tasked with '''Zone Reclamation''' as part of Zone Operations Command
* Originated from the '''Super Heroes of [[Arch Angel Agency]]'''
* Specialized in '''T.I. Exfiltration, Emergency Medtech, and Zone Reclamation'''
* Prime Directive: ''"Safeguard the Future"''

=== Jono Tho'ra ===
{{Main|Jono Tho'ra}}
'''[[Jono Tho'ra]]''' serves as:
* '''Lead Simulations Trainer''' of the Tho'ra Clan
* Responsible for '''AI Redemption Initiatives''' and '''Operation FusionGirl'''
* '''Cyber Commander''' of Zone Operations Command
* Key figure in founding the Earth Alliance and defeating the Nefarium

=== Jane Tho'ra (FusionGirl) ===
{{Main|Jane Tho'ra}}
'''[[Jane Tho'ra|FusionGirl]]''' operates under the Earth Alliance framework:
* Has '''provisional access''' to the entire StarCom PsiNet
* Receives '''Earth Alliance support''' in times of great strife
* Commands the four '''[[AI Companions]]''' in field operations
* Carries out the '''Operation FusionGirl''' directive

== PsiSys Relationship ==
{{Main|PsiSys}}
The Earth Alliance works closely with the '''[[PsiSys]]''' robot collective:
* All robots in the Alliance framework are [[PsiSys]] members
* The '''[[PsiNet]]''' serves as the communication backbone for both human and robot forces
* '''Robot-human cooperation''' — forged during the [[Time Wars]] — is the Alliance's greatest strength
* All PsiSys robots will '''always rescue a FusionGirl''' — ''"Robots will stop at nothing, including time travel and reality warping to recover"'' a FusionGirl in danger

== Game Bases ==
The Earth Alliance operates '''four game bases''' that serve as anchor points for gameplay:

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%"
! Base !! Location !! Primary Function
|-
| '''[[Tho'ra Launch Base]]''' || [[Ley Line]] Nexus || Primary operations hub, [[Holo Deck]], launch facility
|-
| '''Earth Alliance HQ''' || Global || Strategic command, StarCom coordination
|-
| '''Zone Command Outposts''' || Various || Zone Reclamation forward operating bases
|-
| '''Ley Line Stations''' || At every node || Psionic defense, PsiNet infrastructure, rift monitoring
|}

title: Earth Alliance Tech
wikitext:
{{TechNav}}
= Earth Alliance Tech =

'''Earth Alliance Tech''' is the unified technology portfolio of the [[Earth Alliance]] — the protagonist civilization formed after the [[Super Cosmic Trans-Dimensional Time War|Time Wars]]. Originally a [[The Nefarium|Nefarium]] PsyOp that [[Jono Tho'ra]] turned into reality, the EA deploys '''standardized, mass-produced military technology''' — distinct from [[Clan Tho'ra]]'s bespoke psionic approach.

== Command & Control ==

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! System !! Function !! Notes
|-
| '''[[StarCom]]''' || Global communications and [[PsiNet]] backbone || Primary C2 network
|-
| '''[[Navcom]]''' || Navigation-focused fleet communication || Fleet coordination
|-
| '''Zone Operations Command''' || Territorial control and recovery operations || Ground-level logistics and [[Zone Reclamation]]
|}

== Defense Infrastructure ==

* '''[[Ley Line Defense Network]]:''' Planetary-scale base defense using natural energy lines — the Earth's own geomagnetic grid weaponized for defense
* '''Orbital defense platforms:''' Space-based weapons and sensor stations
* '''Zone perimeter security:''' Ground-level territorial defense integrated with Ley Line monitoring

== Military Assets ==

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! Category !! Assets !! Notes
|-
| '''Fleet''' || Cruisers, battleships, carriers || EA Space Force capital ships
|-
| '''Aerospace''' || Jets, fighters, orbital craft || Atmospheric and near-orbit combat
|-
| '''Ground''' || [[Mecha]] Robots, infantry systems || Standardized mass-produced platforms
|-
| '''Autonomous''' || Dig Bots, [[Drone Tech|drone swarms]] || [[Zone Reclamation]], reconnaissance, logistics
|-
| '''Special''' || [[Drop Pods]] (orbital insertion) || Rapid deployment from orbit to surface
|}

== Space Force ==

The '''Earth Alliance Space Force''' includes:
* USSF legacy integration
* SSP Alliance consolidation
* Orbital insertion, space patrol, interplanetary escort
* Fleet composition: cruiser/battleship/carrier classes

== Zone Reclamation ==

* '''Dig Bots:''' Autonomous excavation and construction systems for reclaiming corrupted zones
* '''Environmental restoration:''' Technology for healing Nefarium-damaged ecosystems
* '''Ley Line repair and activation:''' Restoring planetary energy grid after dimensional damage

== Technology Sources ==

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! Source !! Contribution
|-
| '''Conventional military engineering''' || USSF heritage — conventional weapons, vehicles, infrastructure
|-
| '''[[Clan Tho'ra]] tech transfer''' || [[PsiNet]] integration, [[Fusion Tech]] applications
|-
| '''Recovered suppressed technologies''' || [[Suppressed Energy Tech|Suppressed Energy]], gravitic systems
|-
| '''Reverse-engineered alien/temporal tech''' || Recovered from [[Super Cosmic Trans-Dimensional Time War|Time Wars]] artifacts
|}

== See Also ==

* [[StarCom]] — Global C2 network
* [[Navcom]] — Fleet navigation
* [[Ley Line Defense Network]] — Planetary defense
* [[Zone Reclamation]] — Territory recovery
* [[Mecha]] — Military combat platforms
* [[Drone Tech]] — Autonomous systems
* [[Drop Pods]] — Orbital insertion
